feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I'm enjoying watching the entire gaming industry crash and burn. We're only half…
entry.content: I'm enjoying watching the entire gaming industry crash and burn. We're only halfway through 2026. Who knows what the next six months will bring? I'm not even thinking about what 2027 will be like. Isn't it exciting? You keep thinking "Well, it can't get any worse." But it always can get worse. More price hikes? More layoffs? PS6 and Helix will probably cost like $1,300, but at least you still have 2027 to buy physical PlayStation games. Nintendo basically already said fuck physical, and now Play

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Giving thanks to Ganesha
entry.content: Look. I am not Hindu. I am white as Hitler and even more fucked up. But I need to tell the Indian guys here something nice. I saw a simple image of Ganesha and I simply projected my own internal need for obstruction in my life to be removed. What a kind God! He simply offers help, and nothing more than more good turns from you to help others too. With in moments, I watched as my life changed quickly. It was not that it was Ganesha, or just me, it was a psychological meme that I have always appre

entry.title: /b/ Random: Is Furry Culture Gen Z's New Mental Illness?
entry.content: Not a doctor, so I'll get straight to the point. What is the psychological malfunction that makes someone want to act like a four-legged animal? It’s not even about having a pet anymore. They are literally blurring the lines between human affection and animals—or even worse, degenerate stuff. Why is this being normalized as a "quirky hobby" instead of being treated in an asylum? Is this the peak of Gen Z mental decay?

entry.title: /int/ International: If they fell in waist-deep water, why didn't they stand up?
entry.content: Our media doesn't like us asking the wrong questions and likes to label us conspiracy theorists... These cases make zero sense. If I were to fall into a 3-foot deep pool, the first thing I'd do is stand up. I wouldn't go "the water is dirty, my life is ruined, guess I'll die" and let myself drown.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Bored Anons get in here!
entry.content: It's like this, I already tried AI and it would have done what I needed but my Computers all fail to run local AI without massive errors. What I need: I collected a massive amount of Data on various USB Disks (Terrabytes. For the time the Internet isn't accessible anymore or other survival Scenarios. I kinda sorted the stuff well but it's still a mess. What I need is a "Content Management System" that works serverless from USB Disk. needed features: - Media identification (PDF, E-Books, Comics,

entry.title: /mu/ Music: God Bless Tiny Tim
entry.content: If you know Tiny Tim from anything nowadays, it’d be from his cover of “Livin’ in the Sunlight, Lovin’ in the Moonlight”, which was featured in the pilot episode of SpongeBob (or you might know his cover of “Tiptoe Through the Tulips…” which sometimes appears in horror media); I find, however, that just about everything else in this debut album was far more musically interesting. “Daddy, Daddy, What Is Heaven Like?” shows off Tiny Tim’s (real name Herbert Khaury) real vibrato. “The Other Side” h

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: There’s a hundred thousand streets in this city. You don't need to know the rout…
entry.content: There’s a hundred thousand streets in this city. You don't need to know the route. If I drive for you, you give me a time and a place, I give you a five minute window. Anything happens in that five minutes, then I’m yours, no matter what. Anything happens a minute either side of that, and you’re on your own. Do you understand? Your fortune: Very Bad Luck

entry.title: /g/ Technology: One day AI is gonna scrape every ad profile, every tracker, every account you've…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Dumpster Audio Help
entry.content: Found a pair of Mirage OM-5s in the dumpster with the boards also removed but nearby. Basically, everything is there with the exception of one of the subwoofer mesh covers. Initial testing: >I hooked both of them up, sent some sound through them via a good AVR. They sounded great... until the light on the front of each went red after about 30 minutes. Once this happens, perhaps it's in standby mode, but I cannot get the subwoofers to work again like this. >I do a total power off on the tower, wa

entry.title: /o/ Auto: How can you tell how close you are to a wall when you're back parking? I know al…
entry.content: How can you tell how close you are to a wall when you're back parking? I know almost all new cars have back and even front cameras but mine doesn't and it's longer than the average car so how do I park it without looking like an idiot and getting out multiple times from the car? I definitely don't want to make contact with the wall. I haven't done this in a while since when I drive to the shop there's always plenty of space and it's in open air and in my front of my house there's plenty of space
